title: Samantha's Midnight Ride
author: Dady Fredy
subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy

pages.title: Samantha's Midnight Ride
pages.subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy

lines: The stars came out, the crickets sang,
And Dady Fredy's bicycle rang.
"Hop on, Samantha! Hold on tight—
We're riding somewhere magic tonight."
Two bicycles waited at the edge of the lane,
One silver as moonlight, one gold as a flame.
Their wheels were made of stardust and breeze,
Their handlebars wrapped in the leaves of the trees.
They pedaled together down Firefly Road,
Where lanterns of lightning bugs twinkled and glowed.
The path turned to ribbon, the ribbon to light,
And up went their wheels — they were cycling the night.
They rode past the rooftops, they rode past the birds,
Past sleepy volcanoes too dreamy for words.
The clouds made a bike lane of cotton and mist,
And every soft bump was a star that they kissed.
"Look, Dady!" Samantha laughed, starting to spin,
As comets with streamers came racing within.
They rang both their bells — and the moon rang one too,
A silver ding-ding above oceans of blue.
They coasted downhill on a river of sky,
While night-blooming flowers waved softly goodbye.
A family of owls on a branch tipped their wings,
And somewhere far off, sleepy Saturn still swings.
They pedaled through curtains of Northern Light green,
The most glowing colors they'd ever seen.
Their tires left trails of soft shimmering gold,
Like stories too magical ever be told.
But even brave riders grow sleepy and tired,
And even bright wheels must one day be retired.
So softly they glided back down through the air,
With wind in their eyes and the moon in their hair.
They parked both their bicycles under a tree
Where fireflies whispered, "Goodnight… one… two… three…"
And Dady Fredy knelt down and said low,
"Wherever you ride, anak… I will go."
"So close your eyes now, Samantha, my dear…
Dady Fredy is always right here.
Dream of the stars and our bicycles bright—
I'll ride beside you… each and every…night."
Goodnight, Samantha.
Goodnight, anak.
